channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The 11 next big things in AI and data innovations for 2024
item.description: The AI big bang that first caught the world’s attention with ChatGPT is sending energy and innovation out in many directions. Large language models aren’t just chatting and summarizing text: Now they’re also reasoning through problems with increasing autonomy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The 3 next big things in robotics and automation for 2024
item.description: Cars still can’t drive themselves everywhere a human can. Robots can’t replace every human manufacturing worker. But those stubborn facts should not distract us from the interesting and creative work being done to expand the ability of robots and drones to help humans do their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: VA approach to automated software testing is rooted in flexibility and efficiency
item.description: "We have approximately 600 applications in the VA and each product line uses their own different automated tools that suits their needs," Coppinger said. The post VA approach to automated software testing is rooted in flexibility and efficiency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Google might have a radical plan to beat Apple’s iPad – turn ChromeOS into Android and take over the tablet world
item.description: Some new rumors suggest that Google is planning to transform ChromeOS into Android – which could be a big deal for the tablet world.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft brings automated ‘agents’ to M365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new tool in Microsoft 365 Copilot to automate repetitive tasks, part of a drive to make the generative AI (genAI) assistant more useful to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A matter of time: 3 metrics that demonstrate the value of immersion cooling
item.description: Immersion cooling in the data center space gets a lot of positive attention for the formulations that enhance thermal performance, material compatibility, and durability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump appears to be planning to attend SpaceX ‘Starship’ launch scheduled for Tuesday in Texas
item.description: Trump’s visit comes as billionaire Musk has been a near-constant presence at Trump’s side as he builds out his administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump team is seeking to ease US Rules for self-driving cars
item.description: Current federal rules pose significant roadblocks for companies looking to launch vehicles without steering wheels or pedals in large quantities, which Tesla intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Library of Congress says an adversary hacked some emails
item.description: The library said that an adversary accessed email communications during the period from January to September.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: T-Mobile hacked in massive Chinese cyber-espionage: Wall St. Journal
item.description: It was part of a campaign to spy on the phones of high-value intelligence targets, the Journal reports.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senators call for federal probe into Musk's contact with Russia
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators called for a federal investigation into Elon Musk’s involvement in SpaceX’s federal contracts following reports the tech billionaire has been in regular contact with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Patch Tuesday: A Critical Part of Cybersecurity
item.description: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday, the second Tuesday of every month, marks a significant day in cybersecurity as it brings updates and patches for various Microsoft products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EU Develops Guidelines for AI Law Compliance: Gathering Public Feedback
item.description: The European Union collects opinions on defining AI systems and prohibited uses through an online survey, aiming to publish guidelines in early 2025.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google's Gemini and OpenAI's ChatGPT: A Clash of AI Titans
item.description: Explore the battle for dominance between Google's Gemini and OpenAI's ChatGPT in the artificial intelligence landscape.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Exoskeleton helps paralyzed people regain independence
item.description: The WalkON Suit F1, an exoskeleton developed to help people with disabilities, can actually walk over to a person in a wheelchair, solving a major problem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Getting started with Google Password Manager
item.description: If you’re still trying to remember all of your passwords and then type ’em into sites by hand, let me tell you: You’re doing it wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the awe-inspiring 'Aurora' supercomputer at Argonne National Lab
item.description: Argonne National Laboratory's Aurora supercomputer is the fastest in the world, capable of a quintillion calculations per second, and is used to model everything from the structure of the universe to the structure of proteins essential to life.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple announces new feature to help airlines track your lost luggage
item.description: For air travelers who have popped an AirTag in their luggage – but still struggle to get help when suitcases are misplaced – this software update is for you.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI developers discover 'Donald Trump neuron', expert says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
